raw_input:
[4.0/5] If you want to do your own gel nails You will need a nail gel lamp to cure the gel. From my experience with different lamps, leave your nails in the curing lamp for the maximum time. This is quite handy and easy to use. Follow all instructions and you should get great results. Hint: when you go to start your nails, put a coat of Elmer's glue around your skin around the nail and it will keep the gel, base and top coat from sticking to your skin. [5.0/5] Salon Perfect Nails! I just wan't ya'll to know.. I have never given myself a gel polish manicure in my LIFE before yesterday. I had been wanting to for the longest time, but I wasn't 100% prepared to make the commitment to purchasing the light and the top coats and the bottom coats and the paint, etc, etc..

Well if you are on the fence, jump it!

First of all, the lamp works. You set your timer and once you do that, all you have to do is put your hand it and it starts automatically. Awesome! Like a real salon. I was able to do my hands in 45 minutes. It would have been even faster but I did a test run on my thumb before doing them all at once to make sure I didn't mess anything up.

Overall, I am SO happy to have this nail curing lamp! [5.0/5] Easy to use This lamp is super easy to use. After installing the batteries you just turn it on the length of time that you need and you are ready to go. I do dip powder nails, not gel... but everytime I have been to the salon, they have always used a lamp to help cure the top layer faster. This worked exactly the same. Anything that saves me time is a great thing! I would recommend this. the price is great and the product is even better! [4.0/5] Nail salon quality at home My teenager is a nail fanatic, she changes her nail color at least twice a week. She loves using this dryer, it's easy to operate and cuts down on drying time. It was harmless, and it works with all kinds of nail polish, not just Gel. It is really fast and would be great if you are in a hurry. My daughter approves [5.0/5] Great lamp! I really enjoy this UV lamp. It's sleek design and concept make it easy to use. I like the buttons designated for each separate drying time and the LED display is easy to read and understand. It works like a champ and does a great job! I truly think this lamp is worth the price, and I think you'll like it as much as I do. [5.0/5] Love it I love this! Now I can set my nails in no time! No more nicks in my polish because I can't ever seem to let them fully dry before I have to do something! This thing is wonderful! I have only used it with regular nail polish so far, but my daughter has some gel polish I will try next. If anything changes I will update my review. I have a feeling my daughters and I will get plenty of use from this. Nice little machine! [5.0/5] Very Nice I just used this product for the first time just now. I had been using one that I bought 8 years ago, that one was hot on my hands to the point of burning. This one didn't do that at all. Warm not hot. I believe this light is newer technology with LED/UV lights, my old one was UV only. I prefer this one very much so, It is compact and very easy to use. Just simply push the button the corresponds with the time you wish, your hand insert turns it on., turns off automatically with the time selected has run out. Super Easy! I recommend this product for doing any gel polish. [5.0/5] Works great! This is the first UV/LED gel nail polish lamp I've ever tried, but it's been amazing so far! It seems pretty standard, it has options for timing from 10 seconds to 120. The best thing about it is that you can set the setting, and it will remember the setting and turn on when you put your hand in via motion sensors! That's super convenient when you have one or both hands covered in wet polish! It plugs into the wall and has a cord that I've found to definitely be long enough, and it's great that you don't have to rely on batteries for it. 5 stars! [5.0/5] I like it Works great! [4.0/5] Works well- pretty design I love the look of this lamp. It almost looks like it came straight out of a salon from the 50s, but also manages to look very modern. It's relatively small and doesn't take up too much space among my other bathroom appliances beneath the sink. The plug isn't very long, maybe 4 feet, so wherever you do your nails you will need to be sitting near an outlet.

My daughter has sensitive skin, and when she stuck her hand under the lamp she said it bothered her hands a little because of the heat. I don't think the heat was too intense. I have a fresh burn on my finger that didn't feel bothered by the light so I'm attributing that to her being younger and more sensitive to heat, but I do think it's something to be aware of.

My fingernails weren't completely dry when I pulled them out, and I had to run them through twice as many cycles as the nail polish recommended, but honestly I think it was due more to the nail polish I used than it was a fault of the lamps (for reference, it was China Glaze brand Gelaze). The nail polish was really super runny and had a watery consistency with very thin coverage.

Two days later though (a record for two people who are hard on their nails) my daughter and I both have zero chips in our polish. I'm pretty happy to have received this and think it would make a great gift or be a good purchase for those who want to save a little money from getting a professional manicure.

Just one last note: I had to go to a beauty supply store to find a gel polish that needed a lamp. It seems like many gels these days advertise no lamp needed. Personally I feel like those aren't any better than regular polish, but it's something to consider. [5.0/5] Great for gel nail polish! This is just like the ones at the salon! You have the option of setting the timer for 30, 60, or 90 seconds to dry gel polish. I love it and am so glad I got it! [4.0/5] Good Quality Nail Lamp This is a really nice, good looking nail lamp. It's kind of on the luxurious side, and seems nicer than ones that have been used on me at the nail salon. You set a time based on the buttons on the side, and apply pressure to the inner plate. My mother tried it out, and though she had to use it for several minutes per side for her normal drug store nail polish, it did the job. Maybe with gel polish, one minute countdown would do the trick, but not for the normal stuff. Still, cool product, happy to have it. Could probably use it on feet too, if you wanted to. [1.0/5] BURNED OUT IN ONE MONTH So disappointed. Loved it when it worked. I used it twice no problem. Today is my third time and it just QUIT. Now I see it is unavailable for purchase. I guess that means they won't replace it so I am out the money. "Let the buyer beware". Maybe this type of item is better to buy in a store when you can return it then buy another at the same time. [5.0/5] Will recommend! Will buy again! I have had my lamp for so long and I will buy this brand again whenever I need a new lamp! I feel like I have had my lamp for about a year and use it about every two weeks, and it is still in perfect condition. There are no lights burnt out and this thing works wonders! [4.0/5] 54w nail curing lamp Nail curing lamp 54w. I am happy with this nail lamp. I can use it for my hands and my toes. It is quite large and roomy inside. It has multiple settings and the cord is a good length. I like how you can add time with the push of a button on the back. The lamp is made of hard plastic and appears to be durable. I like the rose gold accents it makes it feel just like the ones I use at the salon. [5.0/5] Must have! Product does as it should salon quality! [3.0/5] It’s decent I was led astray by the great reviews. When my product came, it had purple ink all over it and it works but it takes over 5 minutes to cure my gel polish. The “54 W” title I think is a bit exaggerated because I’ve seen a 42 W work better and faster than this one. It’s worth $20 I guess but I would rather invest in a good one. The reviews had me fooled thinking I would get a great product for a cheap price. [5.0/5] Very impressed I am very impressed with this nail lamp.
Looks very high quality and works very good.
It's super fast and easy to work on and  dries my nails perfectly.  [5.0/5] Fabuloso 😍 Estoy muy emocionada con este producto, es una maravilla [5.0/5] Best value for shellac/gel polish lights I was concerned these lights would not be as powerful as the professional brand I had previously been using, but I have been VERY pleased and satisfied with the results!!! Definitely a great purchase!

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, most customers are very happy with this nail curing lamp, with the majority giving it 4 or 5 stars. Many reviewers highlight how easy it is to use, noting that it automatically turns on when you insert your hand and shuts off when the timer runs out. Customers appreciate the multiple timer settings and the clear LED display. Several reviewers mention that it works just like the lamps used in professional salons, and many are thrilled to be able to do salon-quality gel nails at home.

The lamp is praised for being compact, good-looking, and working with various types of nail polish, not just gel. Long-term users report that it holds up well over time with regular use. A few customers note that the cord is on the shorter side, so you need to be near an outlet.

On the downside, there are a couple of complaints. One customer received a unit that stopped working after just three uses, and another felt the wattage was exaggerated, saying it took too long to cure their gel polish. One reviewer also noted that people with sensitive skin may find the heat slightly uncomfortable.

Overall, most buyers feel the lamp offers great value for the price and would recommend it to others looking to do their own gel manicures at home.
